sections.paragraph:
φόρμιγξ (φόρμιγξ, ιγγος,)

the phorminx, a kind of lyre or harp, the oldest stringed instrument of the Greeks, esp. as the instrument of Apollo, Hom.; with seven strings (after Terpander's time), Pind.


Etym. Commonly referred to φέρω, as if it were the portable lyre: better perh. from Root ΦΡΕΜ, Lat. fremo, to sound.
